member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.Simulation
member.summary: Accessing and altering this object is inherently unsupported and unsafe, this is the internal bepu simulation.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.AssociatedScene
member.summary: The scene associated with this simulation.
member.remarks: When this is set, entities spawning inside this scene will be automatically associated with this simulation as long as their is set to . See for more info.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.ThreadCount
member.summary: The number of threads the simulation runs on.
member.remarks: A negative value results in automatic thread selection.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.Enabled
member.summary: Whether to update the simulation.
member.remarks: False also disables contact processing but won't prevent re-synchronization of static physics bodies to their engine counterpart

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.ParallelUpdate
member.summary: Allow entity synchronization to occur across multiple threads instead of just the main thread

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.Deterministic
member.summary: Whether to use a deterministic time step when using multithreading. When set to true, additional time is spent sorting constraint additions and transfers.
member.remarks: This can only affect determinism locally- different processor architectures may implement instructions differently. There is also some performance cost

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.FixedTimeStepSeconds
member.summary: The number of seconds per step to simulate. Lossy, prefer .

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.TimeScale
member.summary: The speed of the simulation compared to real-time.
member.remarks: This stacks with ., changing that one already affects the simulation speed.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.MaxStepPerFrame
member.summary: Represents the maximum number of steps per frame to avoid a death loop

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.UsePerBodyAttributes
member.summary: Allows for per-body features like at a cost to the simulation's performance
member.remarks: will be ignored if this is false.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.PoseGravity
member.summary: Global gravity settings. This gravity will be applied to all bodies in the simulations that are not kinematic.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.PoseLinearDamping
member.summary: Controls linear damping, how fast object loose their linear velocity

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.PoseAngularDamping
member.summary: Controls angular damping, how fast object loose their angular velocity

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.SolverIteration
member.summary: The number of iterations for the solver
member.remarks: Smaller values improve performance at the cost of stability and precision.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.SolverSubStep
member.summary: The number of sub-steps used when solving constraints
member.remarks: Smaller values improve performance at the cost of stability and precision.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.SoftStartDuration
member.summary: The duration for the SoftStart; when the simulation starts up, more run to improve constraints stability and let them come to rest sooner.
member.remarks: Negative or 0 disables this feature.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.SoftStartSubstepFactor
member.summary: Multiplier over during Soft Start

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.FixedTimeStep
member.summary: The amount of time between individual simulation steps/ticks, by default ~16.67 milliseconds which would run 60 ticks per second
member.remarks: Larger values improve performance at the cost of stability and precision.

member.name: M:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.NextUpdate
member.summary: Yields execution until right before the next physics tick
member.returns: Task that will resume next tick.

member.name: M:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.AfterUpdate
member.summary: Yields execution until right after the next physics tick
member.returns: Task that will resume next tick.

member.name: M:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.ShouldPerformPhysicsTest(Stride.BepuPhysics.CollisionMask,BepuPhysics.Collidables.CollidableReference)
member.summary: Whether a physics test with against should be performed or entirely ignored

member.name: M:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.RayCast(Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3@,Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3@,System.Single,Stride.BepuPhysics.HitInfo@,Stride.BepuPhysics.CollisionMask)
member.summary: Finds the closest intersection between this ray and shapes in the simulation.
member.param: The start position for this ray
The normalized direction the ray is facing
The maximum distance from the origin that hits will be collected
An intersection in the world when this method returns true, an undefined value when this method returns false
Which layer should be hit
member.returns: True when the given ray intersects with a shape, false otherwise

member.name: M:Stride.BepuPhysics.BepuSimulation.RayCastPenetrating(Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3@,Stride.Core.Mathematics.Vector3@,System.Single,System.Span{Stride.BepuPhysics.HitInfoStack},Stride.BepuPhysics.CollisionMask)
member.summary: Collect intersections between the given ray and shapes in this simulation.
member.remarks:
When there are more hits than can accomodate, returns only the closest hits.
There are no guarantees as to the order hits are returned in.
member.param: The start position for this ray
The normalized direction the ray is facing
The maximum distance from the origin that hits will be collected

A temporary buffer which is used as a backing array to write to, its length defines the maximum amount of info you want to read. It is used by the returned enumerator as its backing array from which you read
Which layer should be hit

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BodyComponent.SleepThreshold
member.summary: Threshold of squared combined velocity under which the body is allowed to go to sleep. Setting this to a negative value guarantees the body cannot go to sleep without user action.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BodyComponent.MinimumTimestepCountUnderThreshold
member.summary: The number of time steps that the body must be under the sleep threshold before the body becomes a sleeping candidate. Note that the body is not guaranteed to go to sleep immediately after meeting this minimum.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BodyComponent.Awake
member.summary: Whether the body is being actively simulated. Setting this to true will attempt to wake the body; setting it to false will force the body and any constraint-connected bodies asleep.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BodyComponent.LinearVelocity

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BodyComponent.AngularVelocity
member.summary: The rotation velocity in unit per second
member.remarks: The rotation format is in axis-angle, meaning that AngularVelocity.Normalized is the axis of rotation, while AngularVelocity.Length is the amount of rotation around that axis in radians per second

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BodyComponent.Position
member.summary: The position of this body in the physics scene, setting it will teleport this object to the position provided.
member.remarks:
Using this property to move objects around is not recommended, as it disregards any collider that may overlap with the body at this new position, you should make sure the area is clear to ensure this object does not become stuck in the scenery.
This value is slightly offset from this entity's Transform based on its

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BodyComponent.Orientation
member.summary: The rotation of this body in the physics scene, setting it will 'teleport' this object's rotation to the one provided.
member.remarks: Using this property to move objects around is not recommended, as it disregards any collider that may overlap with the body at this new orientation, you should make sure the area is clear to ensure this object does not become stuck in the scenery.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BodyComponent.BodyInertia
member.summary: The mass and inertia tensor of this body

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BodyComponent.SpeculativeMargin
member.summary:
Automatically computed size of the margin around the surface of the shape in which contacts can be generated. These contacts will have negative depth and only contribute if the frame's velocities would push the shapes of a pair into overlap.
This is automatically set by bounding box prediction each frame, and is bound by the collidable's and values. The effective speculative margin for a collision pair can also be modified from callbacks.
This should be positive to avoid jittering.
It can also be used as a form of continuous collision detection, but excessively high values combined with fast motion may result in visible 'ghost collision' artifacts. For continuous collision detection with less chance of ghost collisions, use .
If using , consider setting to a smaller value to help filter ghost collisions.
For more information, see the Continuous Collision Detection documentation.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.BodyComponent.ContinuousDetection
member.summary: Determines the continuous collision detection configuration set for that object. Helps prevent fast-moving objects from tunneling through other objects.

member.name: M:Stride.BepuPhysics.CharacterComponent.GroundTest(System.Numerics.Vector3,System.Single)
member.summary: Returns whether this body is in contact with the ground.
member.remarks: Goes through the list of to do so
member.param: Which direction a surface has to be in to be considered as ground

How close to this direction a supporting contact has to be for it to be considered as ground. In the [-1,1] range, where -1 would return true for any given surface we are in contact with, 0 would return true for a surface that is at most 90 degrees away from , and 1 would return true only when a surface matches exactly.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.CollidableComponent.Simulation
member.summary: The simulation this object belongs to, null when it is not part of a simulation.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.CollidableComponent.Collider
member.summary: The collider definition used by this object.
member.remarks: Changing this value will reset some of the internal physics state of this body

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.CollidableComponent.SpringFrequency
member.summary: The bounce frequency in hz
member.remarks: Must be low enough that the simulation can actually represent it. If the contact is trying to make a bounce happen at 240hz, but the integrator timestep is only 60hz, the unrepresentable motion will get damped out and the body won't bounce as much.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.CollidableComponent.SpringDampingRatio
member.summary: The amount of energy/velocity lost when this collidable bounces off

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.CollidableComponent.MaximumRecoveryVelocity
member.summary: The maximum speed this object will exit out of the collision when overlapping another collidable

member.name: T:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.BallSocketConstraintComponent
member.summary:
Creates a spherical joint (also known as a ball and socket joint) that constrains two bodies to share a connection point.
This constraint keeps a specific point on body A (defined by ) coincident with a specific point on body B (defined by ), while still allowing full rotational freedom around the connection point.
Common uses include:
Character joint connections (shoulders, hips, etc.)
Chain links
Pendulums
Rag doll physics
Cloth and soft body simulation

member.name: T:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.CenterDistanceConstraintComponent
member.summary: Constrains the center of two bodies to be separated by a target distance. This constraint ensures that the distance between the center points of two bodies attempts to match a specific target value. Unlike , this constraint operates directly on the body centers rather than on specific points on the bodies.
member.remarks: This is a specialized variant of that works with body centers. Use this when you need to constrain the distance between bodies without specifying exact attachment points. For a version that allows a range of distances rather than a single target value, see .

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.CenterDistanceConstraintComponent.TargetDistance
member.summary: Target distance between the body centers.

member.name: T:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.CenterDistanceLimitConstraintComponent
member.summary: Constrains the center of two bodies to be separated by a distance within a range. This constraint ensures that the distance between the center points of two bodies remains within a minimum and maximum range. Unlike , this constraint operates directly on the body centers rather than on specific points on the bodies.
member.remarks: This is a specialized variant of that works with body centers. Use this when you need to constrain the overall distance between bodies without specifying exact attachment points.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.CenterDistanceLimitConstraintComponent.MinimumDistance
member.summary: Minimum distance between the body centers.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.CenterDistanceLimitConstraintComponent.MaximumDistance
member.summary: Maximum distance between the body centers.

member.name: T:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.DistanceLimitConstraintComponent
member.summary: Constrains points on two bodies to be separated by a distance within a specified range. This constraint ensures that the distance between two points on two bodies remains within a minimum and maximum range. It is useful for creating elastic or flexible connections between bodies, where the distance can vary within the specified limits.
member.remarks: Unlike , this constraint allows you to specify exact attachment points on each body using and properties. If you need to constrain only the centers of bodies, use instead.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.DistanceLimitConstraintComponent.MinimumDistance
member.summary: Minimum distance permitted between the point on A and the point on B.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.DistanceLimitConstraintComponent.MaximumDistance
member.summary: Maximum distance permitted between the point on A and the point on B.

member.name: T:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.DistanceServoConstraintComponent
member.summary: Constrains points on two bodies to be separated by a target distance using servo settings. This constraint attempts to maintain a specific distance between two points on two bodies by applying forces to reach the target distance. It uses servo settings to control the speed and force applied to achieve the target distance.
member.remarks: Unlike , this constraint allows you to specify exact attachment points on each body using and properties. If you need to constrain only the centers of bodies, use instead. For a version that allows a range of distances rather than a single target value, see .

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.DistanceServoConstraintComponent.TargetDistance
member.summary: Distance that the constraint will try to reach between the attachment points.

member.name: T:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.IMotor
member.summary: Motors try to reach a given relative velocity between the connected bodies

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.IMotor.MotorMaximumForce
member.summary: Maximum amount of force the motor can apply in one unit of time.
member.userdoc: Maximum amount of force the motor can apply in one unit of time.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.IMotor.MotorDamping
member.summary: Mass-scaled damping constant, How rigid the constraint is.
member.remarks: If you want to simulate a viscous damping coefficient of D with an object of mass M, set this damping value to D / M.
member.userdoc: Mass-scaled damping constant, How rigid the constraint is.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.IMotor.MotorSoftness
member.summary: Gets or sets how soft the constraint is. Values range from 0 to infinity. Softness is inverse damping; 0 is perfectly rigid, 1 is very soft, float.MaxValue is effectively nonexistent.

member.name: T:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.IServo
member.summary: Servos change velocity of the connected bodies to achieve a position or orientation goal

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.IServo.ServoMaximumSpeed
member.summary: Maximum speed that the constraint can try to use to move towards the target.
member.userdoc: Maximum speed that the constraint can try to use to move towards the target.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.IServo.ServoBaseSpeed
member.summary: Minimum speed that the constraint will try to use to move towards the target. If the speed implied by the spring configuration is higher than this, the servo will attempt to use the higher speed. Will be clamped by the MaximumSpeed.
member.userdoc: Minimum speed that the constraint will try to use to move towards the target. If the speed implied by the spring configuration is higher than this, the servo will attempt to use the higher speed. Will be clamped by the MaximumSpeed.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.IServo.ServoMaximumForce
member.summary: The maximum force that the constraint can apply to move towards the target.
member.remarks: This value is specified in terms of force: a change in momentum over time. It is approximated as a maximum impulse (an instantaneous change in momentum) on a per-substep basis. In other words, for a given velocity iteration, the constraint's impulse can be no larger than * dt where dt is the substep duration.
member.userdoc: The maximum force that the constraint can apply to move towards the target.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.ISpring.SpringFrequency
member.summary: The target number of undamped oscillations per unit of time. Higher frequency values create stiffer connections, while lower values allow more elasticity in the joint.
member.userdoc: The target number of undamped oscillations per unit of time. Higher frequency values create stiffer connections, while lower values allow more elasticity in the joint.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.ISpring.SpringDampingRatio
member.summary: The ratio of the spring's actual damping to its critical damping. 0 is undamped, 1 is critically damped, and higher values are overdamped. Higher damping ratios reduce oscillations and make the connection less elastic.
member.userdoc: The ratio of the spring's actual damping to its critical damping. 0 is undamped, 1 is critically damped, and higher values are overdamped. Higher damping ratios reduce oscillations and make the connection less elastic.

member.name: T:Stride.BepuPhysics.Constraints.OneBodyAngularServoConstraintComponent
member.summary:
Constrains a single body to a target orientation in space, controlling both rotation and tilt.
This constraint applies torque to make a body gradually align with a specific target orientation. It works like a spring system that pulls the body's orientation (rotation around any axis, including tilt) toward the specified target orientation.
Common uses include:
Stabilizing objects to maintain a specific orientation
Creating motorized joints that rotate/tilt objects to desired angles
Simulating gyroscopic or magnetic orientation control
The spring and servo settings control how quickly and forcefully the body moves toward the target orientation.

member.summary: Local space basis attached to body A against which to measure body B's transformed axis. Expressed as a 3x3 rotation matrix, the X axis corresponds with 0 degrees, the Y axis corresponds to 90 degrees, and the Z axis is the twist axis.
member.userdoc: Local space basis attached to body A against which to measure body B's transformed axis. Expressed as a 3x3 rotation matrix, the X axis corresponds with 0 degrees, the Y axis corresponds to 90 degrees, and the Z axis is the twist axis.

member.name: T:Stride.BepuPhysics.Definitions.CollisionGroup
member.summary: This value follows up when filtering out collisions. It prevents collisions between objects sharing the same , when the absolute difference between their , , and is less than two.
member.remarks:
Example A: You have multiple characters (A, B, C, D) all with the same , they are split in two teams {A, B} and {C, D}, but you don't want members of the same team to collide between each other, you can set {A, B}'s to 1 and {C, D}'s to 2.
Example B: You have a chain of three colliders attached to each other {A, B, C}, you don't want A and C to collide with B, but A and C should collide together. Set A, B and C's Ids to 1 to start filtering, leave A's at 0, B's to 1 and C to 2. A and C will collide since the difference between their is equal to two, but neither of them will collide with B since they are both only one away from B's index.

member.name: F:Stride.BepuPhysics.Definitions.CollisionGroup.Id
member.summary: The identification number used by this object, zero when you don't want to filter more than already does.
member.remarks: Set this value to the same number for all objects which should have their collisions ignored when colliding between each other.

member.name: F:Stride.BepuPhysics.Definitions.CollisionGroup.IndexA
member.summary: An index associated with this object in the collision group
member.remarks: For a bunch of objects sharing the same , if the two objects have the same or subsequent values, collision between those two objects will be ignored. But, if their values are more than one apart, they will collide with each other.

member.summary: Fires when a contact is removed.
member.remarks: This may be called without a corresponding call to , contacts are registered when two collidables are close enough, not necessarily when actually touching. If the two collidables grazed each other, none of the touching methods will be called.
member.typeparam: Type of the contact manifold detected.
member.param: Collidable that the event was attached to.

Set of remaining contacts in the collision.

member.name: T:Stride.BepuPhysics.Definitions.IndexBasedSimulationSelector
member.summary: This selector picks the simulation based on the simulation index

member.name: F:Stride.BepuPhysics.Definitions.InterpolationMode.None
member.summary: No interpolation, the body will be moved on every physics update and left alone during normal updates

member.name: F:Stride.BepuPhysics.Definitions.InterpolationMode.Interpolated
member.summary: The body will move from the previous physics pose to the current physics pose, introducing one physics update of latency but should be very smooth

member.name: F:Stride.BepuPhysics.Definitions.InterpolationMode.Extrapolated
member.summary: The body will move from the current physics pose to a predicted one, reducing the latency but introducing imprecise or jerky motion when the pose changes significantly

member.summary: Implement this to control which a given component is assigned to

member.summary: Go through available simulations and pick the most appropriate one for

member.name: T:Stride.BepuPhysics.Definitions.SceneBasedSimulationSelector
member.summary: This selector picks the simulation based on the scene the entity lives in, see .

remarks.para: - Pick the first simulation whose scene matches the entity's scene.
- If that fails, the first simulation whose scene is null.
- If that failed too, The first simulation in the list.

member.name: F:Stride.BepuPhysics.Definitions.SceneBasedSimulationSelector.Shared
member.summary: A read only instance which you can use instead of newing an instance

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Definitions.StridePoseIntegratorCallbacks.Gravity
member.summary: Gravity to apply to dynamic bodies in the simulation.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Definitions.StridePoseIntegratorCallbacks.LinearDamping
member.summary: Fraction of dynamic body linear velocity to remove per unit of time. Values range from 0 to 1. 0 is fully undamped, while values very close to 1 will remove most velocity.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Definitions.StridePoseIntegratorCallbacks.AngularDamping
member.summary: Fraction of dynamic body angular velocity to remove per unit of time. Values range from 0 to 1. 0 is fully undamped, while values very close to 1 will remove most velocity.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Definitions.StridePoseIntegratorCallbacks.AngularIntegrationMode
member.summary: Gets how the pose integrator should handle angular velocity integration.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Definitions.StridePoseIntegratorCallbacks.AllowSubstepsForUnconstrainedBodies
member.summary: Gets whether the integrator should use substepping for unconstrained bodies when using a substepping solver. If true, unconstrained bodies will be integrated with the same number of substeps as the constrained bodies in the solver. If false, unconstrained bodies use a single step of length equal to the dt provided to Simulation.Timestep.

member.name: P:Stride.BepuPhysics.Definitions.StridePoseIntegratorCallbacks.IntegrateVelocityForKinematics
member.summary: Gets whether the velocity integration callback should be called for kinematic bodies. If true, IntegrateVelocity will be called for bundles including kinematic bodies. If false, kinematic bodies will just continue using whatever velocity they have set. Most use cases should set this to false.

member.name: M:Stride.BepuPhysics.Definitions.StridePoseIntegratorCallbacks.PrepareForIntegration(System.Single)
member.summary: Callback invoked ahead of dispatches that may call into . It may be called more than once with different values over a frame. For example, when performing bounding box prediction, velocity is integrated with a full frame time step duration. During substepped solves, integration is split into substepCount steps, each with fullFrameDuration / substepCount duration. The final integration pass for unconstrained bodies may be either fullFrameDuration or fullFrameDuration / substepCount, depending on the value of AllowSubstepsForUnconstrainedBodies.
member.param: Current integration time step duration.
member.remarks: This is typically used for precomputing anything expensive that will be used across velocity integration.

member.summary: Callback for a bundle of bodies being integrated.
member.param: Indices of the bodies being integrated in this bundle.
Current body positions.
Current body orientations.
Body's current local inertia.
Mask indicating which lanes are active in the bundle. Active lanes will contain 0xFFFFFFFF, inactive lanes will contain 0.
Index of the worker thread processing this bundle.
Durations to integrate the velocity over. Can vary over lanes.
Velocity of bodies in the bundle. Any changes to lanes which are not active by the integrationMask will be discarded.
